name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **TANKER PARADOX SOFTENING — FRONTLINE -1.0% TO $37.70 / BOAT -1.2% TO $42.44 ON MOU SCOOP; VLCC TD3C STEEPEST 1-DAY DROP IN 5+ YEARS ON MOU NEWS ('LEAD BALLOON' — Lloyd's List).** WS408 / ~$407K/day TCE — off ~$420K peak; still record territory. The MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision is being priced in equity even though physical chokepoint persists (5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; AWRP at 0.8-1% still 6-8x baseline). Frontline +33.8% 3M arc unwinding modestly. CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile in Hormuz off Dubai keeps kinetic-tail risk live (CBS attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed); demonstrates operational risk persists despite diplomatic pause. Container freight FBX: Asia-USWC ~$2,127/FEU; Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact, Med-route elevation reflects Hormuz/Suez dynamic). **Reversibility:** if Pakistan-mediated MOU track delivers Iran formal Hormuz reopening within 14-21 days (by May 26), tanker stocks reprice -15-25% (rates collapse to normalized levels). If Iran rejects, tankers re-bid +5-10% on premium re-pricing. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG redeployment Suez→Red Sea/Gulf of Aden adds new naval principal — could over time reduce transit-risk perception.
